global_rules: When implementing a query optimizer in Python, ensure all classes (such as OrCondition, AndCondition, Condition, ColumnRef) referenced in the optimizer are explicitly imported from their defining modules (e.g. query.py).

To evaluate JOIN conditions (especially on self-joins), always call join.condition.evaluate_join(left_row, right_row) instead of join.condition.evaluate(combined). Implement evaluate_join(self, left_row, right_row) in Condition, AndCondition, and OrCondition. In Condition.evaluate_join, resolve left-hand side expression from left_row and right-hand side expression from right_row (with fallback logic using a parameter like prefer_left).

When writing unit tests for LEFT JOIN, calculate the expected row count carefully. For a LEFT JOIN of a left table with 5 rows and a right table with 5 rows: if one left row matches 2 right rows, two left rows match 1 right row each, and two left rows match 0 right rows, the correct expected row count is 6 (2 + 1 + 1 + 1 + 1 = 6), NOT 5. Do not make manual arithmetic errors in test assertions.
